question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: Routes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a POST request to /metrics (which only accepts GET per Routes()) to cause an unexpected HTTP method response, and use the resulting status_code label in hiero_mirror_rosetta_request_duration to probe the routing logic and discover whether /construction/submit has similar method restrictions that could be bypassed?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maximum body size enforcement in MetricsMiddleware to submit an extremely large /construction/submit body, causing the requestBytesHistogram to record an +Inf bucket observation while the server attempts to parse the oversized tampered transaction, potentially causing OOM?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user use the route label values exposed in hiero_mirror_rosetta_request_inflight to confirm that /construction/submit is actively processing a request, then send a duplicate tampered transaction to create a double-spend scenario while the original is still inflight?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that MetricsMiddleware uses middleware.Instrument from weaveworks/common, which may have known vulnerabilities in route matching, to inject arbitrary route label values into hiero_mirror_rosetta_request_duration and corrupt the metrics used to detect tampered transaction submission patterns?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send /construction/submit requests with HTTP methods other than POST (e.g., PUT, PATCH) to generate novel method label values in hiero_mirror_rosetta_request_bytes, polluting the histogram and making it harder for operators to identify the method used to submit a tampered transaction?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the sequential middleware chain (MetricsMiddleware -> TracingMiddleware -> CorsMiddleware) to submit a tampered /construction/submit request that passes CORS checks but whose metrics are recorded before the CORS rejection, creating a phantom metric entry that misleads operators about the number of transaction submissions?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user use the hiero_mirror_rosetta_response_bytes histogram to distinguish between a 200 OK response (larger body with transaction hash) and a 4xx error response (smaller error body) for /construction/submit, using this as an oracle to determine whether a tampered transaction was accepted?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of per-IP rate limiting in MetricsMiddleware to submit thousands of tampered /construction/submit requests per second, with the metrics middleware faithfully recording each one in hiero_mirror_rosetta_request_inflight without triggering any automatic blocking?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user use the publicly accessible /metrics endpoint to monitor hiero_mirror_rosetta_request_duration bucket counts for /construction/submit in real time, detecting when a legitimate user is submitting a transaction and injecting a tampered transaction immediately after to exploit any replay window?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the RouteMatcher cast (next.(middleware.RouteMatcher)) to cause a nil pointer dereference if the router is reconfigured at runtime, crashing the middleware and creating a window during which /construction/submit is unmonitored and tampered transactions can be submitted without metrics recording?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send /construction/submit requests with extremely long URL paths to generate high-cardinality route label values in hiero_mirror_rosetta_request_inflight, causing Prometheus memory exhaustion and disabling the inflight gauge that operators use to detect concurrent tampered transaction submissions?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that MetricsMiddleware wraps the entire router (including /construction/submit) with no transaction-specific integrity checks to submit a /construction/submit request where the transaction body has been modified in transit, with the middleware faithfully recording the tampered request as a normal metric observation?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user use the hiero_mirror_rosetta_request_inflight gauge to detect when the server is processing multiple concurrent /construction/submit requests, then submit a tampered transaction during this high-concurrency window to exploit potential race conditions in transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that MetricsMiddleware does not validate the Content-Type header of /construction/submit requests to submit a tampered transaction with an unexpected Content-Type, bypassing any content-type-based filtering while still being recorded as a normal metric observation?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user use the hiero_mirror_rosetta_response_bytes histogram to distinguish between different error types returned by /construction/submit (e.g., ErrTransactionUnmarshallingFailed vs ErrTransactionSubmissionFailed) based on response body size differences, learning which tampered transaction structures reach the network submission stage?
